name: raycast-backup-sync
title: Raycast Backup & Sync
description: Back up Raycast's local data to your own Google Drive as versioned archives, and restore them on the same machine.
author: rakibulism

commands.name: backup-to-drive
commands.title: Backup Raycast to Google Drive
commands.subtitle: Raycast Backup & Sync
commands.description: Snapshot Raycast's data files and upload a versioned archive to Google Drive.

commands.name: restore-from-drive
commands.title: Restore Raycast from Google Drive
commands.subtitle: Raycast Backup & Sync
commands.description: List backups for this device and restore one onto this machine.

commands.name: manage-backups
commands.title: Manage Raycast Backups
commands.subtitle: Raycast Backup & Sync
commands.description: Browse, inspect, and delete backups stored in Google Drive.

preferences.name: googleClientID
preferences.title: Google OAuth Client ID
preferences.description: OAuth 2.0 Client ID from your Google Cloud project (Web application type, with redirect URI https://raycast.com/redirect).

preferences.name: googleClientSecret
preferences.title: Google OAuth Client Secret
preferences.description: Required for a Web-application OAuth client. Stored securely on this device.

preferences.name: deviceName
preferences.title: Device Name
preferences.description: Folder name used for this machine's backups in Drive. Defaults to the computer's hostname.

preferences.name: keepBackupCount
preferences.title: Backups to Keep
preferences.description: How many recent backups to keep per device. Older ones are deleted after each successful backup. Use 0 to keep all.

preferences.name: backupActivities
preferences.title: Include Clipboard History
preferences.label: Back up the activities database (clipboard history)
preferences.description: The activities database can contain sensitive content you have copied. Disable to exclude it.

preferences.name: warnIfRunning
preferences.title: Safety Checks
preferences.label: Warn / offer to quit Raycast for a consistent snapshot
preferences.description: Raycast should be quit (or its databases checkpointed) for a fully consistent backup and a safe restore.
